programmes.show_description: Louis visits the city of Phoenix, Arizona to observe two cases of love being tested by a condition that steadily erodes the personality and character.
Louis Theroux explores relationships complicated by medical conditions. Here, he visits one of America's best schools for autism.

Popular property renovation series. Tired of squeezing into a bachelor pad and ready to move to the suburbs, Erin and Adam are searching for the perfect family-friendly home.
Drew and Jonathan Scott help another couple in the property renovation series. Jaeme and Bliss need to find their dream home fast, but the odds are stacked against them.
Carbis Bay: Nick Knowles and the team enlist the help of hundreds of volunteers to renovate the home of three orphaned boys living with their grandparents in Carbis Bay, Cornwall.
Tatiana, Peppa & Luna: In a festive special, we meet a crossbreed rescue puppy with severely deformed legs, a Dalmatian with bulging discs in her neck, and a Labrador with painful elbows.
A truck driver is trapped in his crushed cab after a motorway smash, while in Hull, firefighters handcuff a man to the rails of the Humber Bridge to stop him from jumping.
New. My City: Pride suspects a mole is among the ranks, as NCIS and local law enforcement continue to track the Broussard Syndicate and a potentially city-destroying threat.
New. Details: Sherlock discovers that Joan has been lying to him - but it seems that she's not the only one, when Detective Bell becomes the chief suspect in a shooting case.
Emergency nurses fight to revive a woman in cardiac arrest; a patient with terminal lung failure undergoes a risky lung transplant and a breech birth has midwife Lucy braced for resuscitation.

Bangor: For the first time ever, the DIY SOS team head to Bangor in Northern Ireland, the childhood hometown of our build manager Mark Miller, to help the McCreight family.
Corby: The team are in Corby to complete a build for Jackie, Colin and their three children, one of whom suffers from cerebral palsy and epilepsy.

Popular property renovation series. Sarah and Kevin spent years renovating their tiny home, but a baby boy and changing priorities means they are desperate for a suburban home.
The hit property renovation series hosted by Drew and Jonathan Scott. The duo help Tyler and Lindsay, who need a family home where they can put down roots.
Bangor: For the first time ever, the DIY SOS team head to Bangor in Northern Ireland, the childhood hometown of our build manager Mark Miller, to help the McCreight family.
Koa, Kwanda & Cleo: Rescue dog Cleo has struggled with pain in her back leg for years after two failed hip replacement operations, and a puppy from Cornwall is in a huge amount of pain.
Unboxing My House: After a pandemic and two kids, the Wong family's home is no longer the warm and inviting place it once was. Jessica and Lizzy help them de-clutter and reclaim their home.
Bristol: The DIY SOS team and volunteers in Bristol help to transform the home of 24-year-old Ryan, who has spent the last five years in hospital 50 miles away from his family.
Telford: In Shropshire, three schoolchildren, the DIY SOS team and hundreds of volunteers help 12-year-old Matthew return home from hospital for the first time in three years.

programmes.show_name: Katie Piper: Locked Up In Louisiana
programmes.show_description: Katie meets Kia, who is accused of attempted murder after shooting a man while working as a security guard. She claims self-defence, but Katie asks if she used reasonable force.
